verses.1: It was for freedom that Christ set us free; therefore keep standing firm and do not be subject again to a yoke of slavery.
verses.2: Look! I, Paul, tell you that if you have yourselves circumcised, Christ will be of no benefit to you.
verses.3: And I testify again to every man who has himself circumcised, that he is obligated to keep the whole Law.
verses.4: You have been severed from Christ, you who are seeking to be justified by the Law; you have fallen from grace.
verses.5: For we, through the Spirit, by faith, are waiting for the hope of righteousness.
verses.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything, but faith working through love.
verses.7: You were running well; who hindered you from obeying the truth?
verses.8: This persuasion did not come from Him who calls you.
verses.9: A little leaven leavens the whole lump of dough.
verses.10: I have confidence in you in the Lord, that you will adopt no other view; but the one who is disturbing you will bear the punishment, whoever he is.
verses.11: But as for me, brothers and sisters, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been eliminated.
verses.12: I wish that those who are troubling you would even emasculate themselves.
verses.13: For you were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only do not turn your freedom into an opportunity for the flesh, but serve one another through love.
verses.14: For the whole Law is fulfilled in one word, in the statement, “YOU SHALL LOVE YOUR NEIGHBOR AS YOURSELF.”
verses.15: But if you bite and devour one another, take care that you are not consumed by one another.
verses.16: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not carry out the desire of the flesh.
verses.17: For the desire of the flesh is against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; for these are in opposition to one another, in order to keep you from doing whatever you want.
verses.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the Law.
verses.19: Now the deeds of the flesh are evident, which are: sexual immorality, impurity, indecent behavior,
verses.20: idolatry, witchcraft, hostilities, strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, selfish ambition, dissensions, factions,
verses.21: envy, drunkenness, carousing, and things like these, of which I forewarn you, just as I have forewarned you, that those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
verses.22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
verses.23: gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.
verses.24: Now those who belong to Christ Jesus crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
verses.25: If we live by the Spirit, let’s follow the Spirit as well.
verses.26: Let’s not become boastful, challenging one another, envying one another.
